challenges.title: Ghost in the Socials
challenges.scenario: A hacker known as 'v0id_walker' was seen bragging about a breach on an underground forum. We need to find their real username used on professional platforms.
challenges.objective: Find the professional username of 'v0id_walker'. (Hint: He might have a GitHub or LinkedIn with a slightly different name)
hints: Check the profile bio for clues.
challenges.solution: Searching for 'v0id_walker' leads to a GitHub profile where the bio mentions 'Contact me at walker_tech'. The flag is DWM{walker_tech_2026}.

challenges.title: Hidden in Plain Sight
challenges.scenario: An informant sent us a photo of a secret meeting location. They claim the GPS coordinates are hidden inside the image file itself.
challenges.objective: Download the image and extract the hidden metadata to find the secret city.
hints: Look at the 'Artist' or 'Copyright' tag if GPS is missing.
challenges.solution: Extracting EXIF data from the image reveals 'Artist: Mumbai Cyber Hub'. The flag is DWM{mumbai_cyber_hub}.

challenges.title: The Invisible Script
challenges.scenario: Our monitoring system intercepted a suspicious banner ad. Our analysts suspect there's a hidden message encoded in the pixels.
challenges.objective: Analyze the image for hidden steganographic messages.
hints: Check the end of the file (EOF) for appended data.
challenges.solution: Running 'strings' or hex analysis on the image reveals a hidden string at the end of the file: 'DWM{pixel_perfect_spy}'.

challenges.title: Breach Collector
challenges.scenario: A database dump from a recent leak contains a list of 'Secure' users. One specific user 'admin_alpha' has a weak hash.
challenges.objective: Crack the MD5 hash for 'admin_alpha' in the provided breach snippet.
hints: Use a common wordlist or an online cracker.
challenges.solution: The CSV contains 'admin_alpha,5f4dcc3b5aa765d61d8327deb882cf99'. Cracking the MD5 hash reveals 'password123'. Flag: DWM{p@ssword123}.

challenges.title: Log Hunter
challenges.scenario: A server reported multiple failed login attempts. The sysadmin saved the access logs for us to investigate.
challenges.objective: Find the Base64 encoded payload hidden in the log entries.
hints: Identify a string that looks like Base64.
challenges.solution: Scanning the logs reveals an entry: GET /login?debug=RFdN{bG9nX2luamVjdGlvbl9kZXRlY3RlZH0=. Decoding the value leads to the flag.
